title: Blueberry pie, double-crust
subtitle: Fresh blueberries thickened with cornflour under a pastry top
excerpt: A double-crust American blueberry pie: fresh blueberries with sugar, lemon and cornflour, sealed between two layers of shortcrust and baked until the juices bubble through the vent. One 23 cm pie, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Fannie Farmer, The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book (1896), Project Gutenberg: documents American summer-fruit pies in the form used here. Mary Lincoln, Mrs. Lincoln's Boston Cook Book (1884), Project Gutenberg: the cornflour-thickened blueberry filling. New England household manuals (pre-1928, public domain): document the wild-blueberry pie as a regional staple.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze assembled unbaked pie; bake from frozen, adding 15 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Pastry holds in the fridge 2 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven — drops to 180°C after 20 minutes

content.text: Blueberry pie carries one risk: a watery centre. Blueberries hold a lot of juice and release it all in the bake. The cornflour does the thickening work; do not skimp on it.

Fresh blueberries are best, frozen are fine — bake from frozen and add an extra 10 minutes. Wild blueberries give the deepest colour and flavour; cultivated supermarket blueberries are slightly milder but work cleanly.

Rub the {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, first {{caster-sugar}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Add the {{water}} a tablespoon at a time and bring together. Divide in two (one slightly larger), wrap flat, and chill an hour.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Mix the {{blueberries}}, second {{caster-sugar}}, {{cornflour}}, zest and juice of the {{lemon}} and {{cinnamon-ground}} in a bowl. Roll the larger pastry disc and line the pie dish. Tip the filling in, doming slightly. Roll the second disc and lay over the top; seal and crimp the edges. Cut five short slashes from the centre out as steam vents.

Brush with the beaten {{eggs}} and scatter with {{demerara-sugar}}. Set on a tray. Bake at 200°C fan for 20 minutes, then drop to 180°C fan for another 30–35 minutes; the pastry should be deep gold and the filling should bubble through the vents. Cool 30 minutes before slicing.
